title: Beadlea trifasciata
taxonomy: Beadlea trifasciata is classified within the family Apocynaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is part of the order Gentianales, which also includes other plant families such as Loganiaceae and Rubiaceae. Beadlea trifasciata is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it grows in humid environments with high levels of rainfall. The exact classification of Beadlea trifasciata is further detailed within the subfamily Rauvolfioideae, which comprises about 2,000 species. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics.
uses: Beadlea trifasciata is a plant species known for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Pseudobombax ellipticum var. trifasciata is a plant that belongs to the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the hibiscus or mallow family. It is native to tropical regions and is often used in traditional medicine.

The health benefits of Pseudobombax ellipticum var. trifasciata include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, prevent infections, and protect against oxidative stress.

The bioactive constituents of Pseudobombax ellipticum var. trifasciata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Pseudobombax ellipticum var. trifasciata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and skin irritations.
